welcome.title: Welcome to Workflow
welcome.desc: Workflow is a visual AI pipeline editor. Drag nodes onto the canvas, connect them, and run automated AI processing flows — from image generation to video enhancement and beyond.

nodePaletteBtn.title: Node Palette
nodePaletteBtn.desc: Click this button to open the Node Palette — your toolbox of all available nodes.

nodePalette.title: Node Categories
nodePalette.desc:
Nodes are organized into 5 categories:
• AI Model — Run any WaveSpeed AI model
• Input — Upload media files or enter text
• Output — Export files or preview results
• Processing — Combine or select outputs
• Free Tools — 12 built-in tools (background removal, face enhancement, format conversion, etc.)

Drag a node to the canvas or click to add it.

aiTask.title: Image Generator Node
aiTask.desc: The Image Generator node is the core of Workflow — it can run any AI model on the platform. When you select a model, the node card expands to show all configurable parameters inline. After execution, results are also displayed directly inside the node.

features.modelSwitch: Choose from hundreds of AI models — parameters update dynamically based on the selected model
features.dynamicParams: Each model has its own parameters (prompt, image size, seed, etc.) that appear automatically inside the node when selected
features.costEstimate: Real-time cost estimation per execution, so you always know the price before running
features.upstream: Connect upstream node outputs (images, text) as inputs — build multi-step AI pipelines

concat.title: Concat Node — Merge Multiple Outputs
concat.desc:
Need to pass multiple images into an "images" input? Use the Concat node:

1. Connect each upstream node's image output to Concat's value1, value2, value3, etc.
2. Connect Concat's output (now an array) to the downstream node's images parameter.

Example:
[Upload A] → image → Concat → output (array) → [AI Task].images
[Upload B] → image ↗

This works for any type of output — images, videos, text — whenever you need to combine multiple single values into one array input.

directoryTrigger.title: Directory Trigger — Batch Process Local Files
directoryTrigger.desc:
Automatically scan a local folder and run the workflow once per file:

• Pick a directory and file type (Images, Videos, Audio, or All)
• The trigger finds all matching files and feeds them one-by-one into the downstream pipeline
• Great for batch processing — e.g. enhance every photo in a folder, convert all videos, etc.

Each execution receives a single file, so downstream nodes work exactly the same as with a manual upload.

httpTrigger.title: HTTP Trigger — Run Workflows via API
httpTrigger.desc:
Turn any workflow into an HTTP API endpoint:

• Define output fields (e.g. image, prompt) — each becomes an output port on the canvas
• When a POST request arrives, the JSON body fields are extracted and passed to downstream nodes
• Pair with the HTTP Response node to return results back to the caller

This lets you integrate workflows into external apps, scripts, or automation tools by simply sending an HTTP request.

group.title: Group Node — Organize Sub-Workflows
group.desc:
Group multiple nodes into a single collapsible container:

• Drag nodes into a Group to encapsulate a sub-workflow
• Expose selected inputs/outputs on the Group's surface so external nodes can connect to them
• Click "Edit Subgraph" to enter the Group and edit its internal nodes
• Import an existing workflow into a Group to reuse it as a building block

Groups keep complex workflows clean and modular — think of them as functions you can wire together.

canvas.title: Canvas Interactions
canvas.desc:
The canvas is your workspace:
• Drag nodes to position them
• Drag from output ports to input ports to create connections
• Click a node to select it — parameters expand inside the node for editing
• Right-click for context menu (copy, paste, delete)
• Scroll to zoom, drag background to pan
• Shortcuts: Ctrl+Z undo, Ctrl+C/V copy-paste, Delete to remove

canvasTools.title: Canvas Tools
canvasTools.desc:
Right-side toolbar on the canvas:
• Grid — Toggle background grid display
• Select / Hand — Switch between selecting nodes and panning the canvas
• Zoom in / Zoom out
• Fit View — Auto-zoom to fit all nodes in the viewport
• Auto Layout — Automatically arrange nodes in a clean, readable layout

run.title: Run Your Workflow
run.desc:
Click "Run" to execute the entire pipeline:
• Set a run count (×1 to ×99) for batch processing
• Cancel anytime with the stop button
• Click the clock icon to open the Execution Monitor and track real-time progress
• Each node shows its status: queued → running → done

moreMenu.title: Save, Import & Export
moreMenu.desc:
Click the ⋮ menu:
• Save — Save the current workflow
• Save as Template — Save the workflow as a reusable template
• Import / Export — Share workflows via JSON files

promptOptimizer.textPlaceholder: Text to expand or use as context...

imageDialog.title: Optimize with Image
imageDialog.enterTextOrImage: Please enter text or provide an image
